claims.text: BTZSC is a benchmark comprising 22 public datasets for zero-shot text classification.
claims.source_quote: we introduce BTZSC, a comprehensive benchmark of 22 public datasets spanning sentiment, topic, intent, and emotion classification

claims.text: The BTZSC benchmark spans sentiment, topic, intent, and emotion classification tasks.
claims.source_quote: a comprehensive benchmark of 22 public datasets spanning sentiment, topic, intent, and emotion classification

claims.text: The BTZSC benchmark captures diverse domains, class cardinalities, and document lengths.
claims.source_quote: capturing diverse domains, class cardinalities, and document lengths

claims.text: The systematic comparison in BTZSC encompasses four major model families: NLI cross-encoders, embedding models, rerankers, and instruction-tuned LLMs.
claims.source_quote: we conduct a systematic comparison across four major model families, NLI cross-encoders, embedding models, rerankers and instruction-tuned LLMs

claims.text: The BTZSC evaluation encompasses 38 public and custom checkpoints.
Qwen3-Reranker-8B achieves a macro F1 score of 0.72, setting a new state-of-the-art among the evaluated models.
claims.source_quote: modern rerankers, exemplified by Qwen3-Reranker-8B, set a new state-of-the-art with macro F1 = 0.72

claims.text: Modern rerankers set a new state-of-the-art for zero-shot text classification on the BTZSC benchmark.
claims.source_quote: modern rerankers, exemplified by Qwen3-Reranker-8B, set a new state-of-the-art with macro F1 = 0.72

claims.text: GTE-large-en-v1.5 substantially closes the accuracy gap with top-performing models while offering the best trade-off between accuracy and latency.
claims.source_quote: strong embedding models such as GTE-large-en-v1.5 substantially close the accuracy gap while offering the best trade-off between accuracy and latency

claims.text: Instruction-tuned LLMs at 4-12B parameters achieve macro F1 scores up to 0.67 on the BTZSC benchmark.
claims.source_quote: instruction-tuned LLMs at 4--12B parameters achieve competitive performance (macro F1 up to 0.67)

claims.text: Instruction-tuned LLMs excel particularly on topic classification compared to other task types.
claims.source_quote: instruction-tuned LLMs at 4--12B parameters achieve competitive performance (macro F1 up to 0.67), excelling particularly on topic classification

claims.text: Instruction-tuned LLMs trail specialized rerankers in zero-shot text classification performance.
claims.source_quote: excelling particularly on topic classification but trailing specialized rerankers

claims.text: NLI cross-encoders plateau in performance even as backbone size increases.
claims.source_quote: NLI cross-encoders plateau even as backbone size increases

claims.text: Scaling model size primarily benefits rerankers and LLMs over embedding models for zero-shot text classification.
claims.source_quote: scaling primarily benefits rerankers and LLMs over embedding models

claims.text: Existing evaluations such as MTEB often incorporate labeled examples through supervised probes or fine-tuning, leaving genuine zero-shot capabilities underexplored.
claims.source_quote: Existing evaluations, such as MTEB, often incorporate labeled examples through supervised probes or fine-tuning, leaving genuine zero-shot capabilities underexplored

claims.text: Early approaches to zero-shot text classification have predominantly relied on cross-encoder models fine-tuned for natural language inference.
claims.source_quote: early approaches have predominantly relied on cross-encoder models fine-tuned for natural language inference (NLI)

claims.text: BTZSC and accompanying evaluation code are publicly released.
claims.source_quote: BTZSC and accompanying evaluation code are publicly released to support fair and reproducible progress in zero-shot text understanding
